name: IMemoryProvider
description: Contract for episodic memory provider implementations. Provides key-value storage with semantic search capability and episode tracking. Used for workflow memory persistence, context recall, and experience-based learning patterns.

methods.name: get

params.name: key
params.description: Unique key identifying the memory entry

methods.description: Retrieve a memory entry by key. Returns null if the key does not exist. The value type depends on what was stored.

methods.name: set

params.name: key
params.description: Unique key for the memory entry

params.name: value
params.description: Value to store. Must be serializable.

methods.description: Store or update a memory entry. Overwrites any existing value for the same key.

methods.name: search

params.name: query
params.description: Natural language search query for semantic similarity matching

params.name: top_k
params.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 5.

methods.description: Search memory entries by semantic similarity to the query. Returns up to top_k results ordered by relevance score (highest first).

methods.name: list_episodes

params.name: workflow_id
params.description: Unique identifier of the workflow to list episodes for

methods.description: List all episodes (execution memory snapshots) for a workflow. Episodes capture the memory state at key execution points for replay and learning.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
All methods are async — storage and search backends may involve I/O
MemoryEntry contains: key (str), value (Any), score (float), metadata (dict)
Episode contains: workflow_id (str), episode_id (str), inputs (dict), outputs (dict), duration_ms (float), created_at (float), metadata (dict)
get() must return null (not raise) for unknown keys
set() must be idempotent — storing the same key twice overwrites the previous value
search() must return results ordered by relevance score (highest first)
search() with no matches must return an empty list (not raise)
top_k defaults to 5 if not specified
Values stored via set() must be retrievable via get() with the same key
